document.title: Git Yard Core Specification (Lossless Deletion Model)
document.purpose: Define Git Yard as a system for evaluating and executing safe deletion of local Git repositories, with explicit, non-interactive override semantics.

mission.summary: Provide certainty about whether a local Git repository can be deleted without data loss.
mission.core-principle: Never perform destructive actions unless explicitly forced by the user.
mission.primary-question: Will I lose anything if I delete this repository?

environment.description: Root directory containing all managed Git repositories. Scanning is restricted to one directory level.

local-repo.description: Represents a Git repository located in the Git Yard root.

path.description: Absolute filesystem path to the repository root.

repo_id.description: Canonical GitHub identity (owner/repo). Null if no GitHub remote is detected.

repo_name.description: Directory name of the repository (basename of path).

remotes.description: Mapping of remote names to URLs.

is_dirty.description: True if working tree has uncommitted changes.

ahead_count.description: Number of commits ahead of remote.

has_untracked_files.description: True if untracked files are present.

last_commit_time.description: ISO timestamp of most recent commit.

editable_install_detected.description: True if this repository is referenced by a Python editable install (pip install -e).

editable_install_sources.description: Paths to .egg-link or .pth files referencing this repo.

notes: repo_id represents identity; path represents embodiment.
Multiple local repositories may share the same repo_id.
Repositories without repo_id are treated as protected.

github-repo.description: Represents a repository known to exist on GitHub.

deletion-analysis.description: Derived evaluation of whether a repository can be safely deleted.

reasons.description: Human-readable reasons explaining the safety classification.

rules: If repo_id is null → delete_safety = protected-local-only
If ahead_count > 0 or is_dirty → delete_safety = unsafe-local-changes
If repo exists on GitHub AND ahead_count == 0 AND is_dirty == false → delete_safety = safe

commands.name: scan-local
commands.description: Scan Git Yard root (one level deep)

commands.name: analyze
commands.description: Compute deletion safety for all local repositories

force.delete.description: Must be set to 1 to override deletion safety checks.

design-principles: Local-only repositories are protected by default
GitHub is treated as a backup reference, not authority
User retains full control at all times
